subject: Public Health
title: Wasting and Acute Malnutrition
description: One-minute skill: Wasting and Acute Malnutrition.

hook.text: A wasted child is dangerously thin—their body consuming its own muscle to survive starvation.

buildup.text: Wasting means low weight for height. It strikes during famines, conflicts, and economic crises.

discovery.text: Ready-to-use therapeutic food—peanut paste packets—can treat severe wasting without hospitalization.

twist.text: 45 million children under five suffer from wasting—yet less than 25% receive any treatment.

climax.text: Wasting kills directly. A severely wasted child is 12 times more likely to die than a well-nourished one.

punchline.text: A packet of peanut paste can save a child's life.

quiz.question: What is ready-to-use therapeutic food commonly made from?
